 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v5nv","title":"Adapt cass QueryCache and dedup for frankensearch hit types","description":"TRACK: cass migration (Track 1B)
PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensearch Integration

CORRECTED SCOPE (2026-02-27 pass-4 deep audit):

CachedHit wraps cass's internal SearchHit struct (query.rs:876), NOT frankensearch types.
SearchHit is a cass-defined output type that remains unchanged regardless of which search backend produces it. The cache and dedup layers sit ABOVE the frankensearch pipeline — they operate on cass's own result types.

VERIFIED STATE:
- CachedHit (query.rs:1488): wraps SearchHit + lowercase content + bloom filter
- CacheShards (query.rs:1515): LRU cache keyed on query string, values are Vec<CachedHit>
- deduplicate_hits(): operates on Vec<SearchHit>, uses content_hash (u64) for dedup
- is_tool_invocation_noise(): pattern-based content filtering on String — no type dependency

WHAT ACTUALLY NEEDS CHANGING (minimal):
The migration from tantivy to frankensearch does NOT change the SearchHit type. The only changes are:

1. QueryCache embedding storage — currently stores Vec<f32> from SyncEmbed. This ALREADY uses frankensearch's SyncEmbed (embedder.rs:21 pub use frankensearch::SyncEmbed as Embedder). No change needed.

2. CacheShards invalidation — if SyncTwoTierSearcher (Phase 2 optional enhancement) changes result ordering, cached results would be stale. Add a cache_version field that increments when search backend changes. LOW PRIORITY.

3. deduplicate_hits() doc_id parsing — currently parses doc_id format embedded by cass indexer. If frankensearch changes the doc_id format, dedup parser needs updating. But cass controls the doc_id format during indexing, so this is unlikely to change.

CONCLUSION: This bead is NEARLY A NO-OP for the core migration. The cache and dedup layers are already compatible with frankensearch because they operate on cass-internal types (SearchHit, CachedHit), not on frankensearch types (VectorHit, ScoredResult).

REMAINING WORK (small):
- Verify QueryCache.get_embedding() still works after any import changes from uc0qs/ltbab
- Add cache_version field for future-proofing (optional)
- Run existing search_caching.rs tests to verify no regression

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v7","title":"P2.1 Extend ScanContext with scan roots","description":"# Extend ScanContext with Multi-Root Support

## Context
Currently ScanContext has just data_root (single path) and since_ts. For remote support, we need multiple scan roots, each with provenance.

## Location
src/connectors/mod.rs (ScanContext definition)
src/indexer/mod.rs (ScanContext usage)

## Current Structure
\`\`\`rust
pub struct ScanContext {
    pub data_root: PathBuf,
    pub since_ts: Option<i64>,
}
\`\`\`

## New Structure

### Option A: Add scan_roots alongside data_root (backward compatible)
\`\`\`rust
pub struct ScanContext {
    /// Primary data directory (cass internal state)
    pub data_dir: PathBuf,
    
    /// Scan roots to search for agent logs
    /// If empty, connectors use their default detection logic
    pub scan_roots: Vec<ScanRoot>,
    
    /// High-water mark for incremental indexing
    pub since_ts: Option<i64>,
}

pub struct ScanRoot {
    /// Path to scan (e.g., ~/.claude, or /data/remotes/work-laptop/mirror/home/.claude)
    pub path: PathBuf,
    
    /// Provenance for conversations found under this root
    pub origin: Origin,
    
    /// Optional platform hint (affects path interpretation)
    pub platform: Option<Platform>,
    
    /// Optional path rewrite rules (src_prefix -> dst_prefix)
    pub workspace_rewrites: Vec<(String, String)>,
}
\`\`\`

### Option B: Keep ScanContext simple, pass provenance separately
Keep ScanContext as-is, but indexer tracks provenance externally and injects after scan.

## Recommendation
Option A is cleaner - ScanRoot bundles everything needed for a scan pass.

## Migration Path for Connectors
Most connectors currently check ctx.data_root for tests/overrides. Update them to:
1. If scan_roots is non-empty, iterate over matching roots
2. If scan_roots is empty, use existing default detection logic (maintains backward compat)

## Dependencies
- P1.1 (Origin type)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ScanContext extended with scan_roots
- [ ] ScanRoot struct defined with provenance
- [ ] Backward compatibility: empty scan_roots = old behavior
- [ ] At least one connector updated to use new structure
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:06.602228Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T15:36:13.711982Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T15:36:13.711982Z","close_reason":"Added ScanRoot struct with origin, platform, workspace_rewrites. -{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1vkfr","title":"[MEDIUM] reality-check: robot-docs paths advertises <data-dir>/cass.log but only cass tui creates it","description":"## Claim (\`cass robot-docs paths\`)
\`\`\`
paths:
  data dir default: /home/ubuntu/.local/share/coding-agent-search
  db path default: /home/ubuntu/.local/share/coding-agent-search/agent_search.db
  log path: <data-dir>/cass.log (daily rolling)
  trace: user-provided path (JSONL).
\`\`\`

The \`log path\` line reads as a universal property of cass — wherever cass runs, it writes \`<data-dir>/cass.log\` with daily rolling. Agents reading the robot-docs output will expect this file to exist after ANY cass invocation and use it for triage.

## Reality
Log-file initialization is gated on \`Commands::Tui\` in src/lib.rs:2954–2970:

\`\`\`rust
match &command {
    Commands::Tui { data_dir, .. } => {
        let log_dir = data_dir.clone().unwrap_or_else(default_data_dir);
        std::fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir).ok();
        let file_appender = tracing_appender::rolling::daily(&log_dir, \"cass.log\");
        ...
    }
    ...
}
\`\`\`

Other variants (\`Search\`, \`Index\`, \`Stats\`, \`Health\`, \`Diag\`, \`Status\`, \`Doctor\`, \`Capabilities\`, \`Sessions\`, \`Export\`, \`Models\`, \`Timeline\`, \`Expand\`, \`View\`, \`ApiVersion\`, \`Introspect\`, \`RobotDocs\`, \`Sources\`, \`Analytics\`) do NOT hook up the rolling file appender — they only configure the stderr subscriber. No cass.log is produced.

Experimental confirmation:

\`\`\`bash
# Fresh HOME, cass tui --once → log appears:
XDG_DATA_HOME=\$TMP HOME=\$TMP TUI_HEADLESS=1 cass tui --once
find \$TMP -name 'cass.log*'
# /tmp/.../coding-agent-search/cass.log.2026-04-23

# Fresh HOME, cass index --full + cass search → NO log:
XDG_DATA_HOME=\$TMP2 HOME=\$TMP2 cass index --full
XDG_DATA_HOME=\$TMP2 HOME=\$TMP2 cass search matrix --robot
find \$TMP2 -name 'cass.log*'
# (empty)
\`\`\`

Also note: \`src/lib.rs:13620\` emits the user-facing hint \"check cass.log for details\" from \`cass doctor\`, even though \`cass doctor\` doesn't produce a cass.log either.

## Impact
Agents running cass in pure robot mode (never touching the TUI) will never find the file that robot-docs points them at. Triage flows that depend on \"consult cass.log\" silently have no artifact to consult — operators end up running \`cass -v\` or \`CASS_TRACE_FILE=...\` instead.

## Suggested fix
Two options:

1. **Docs fix (easy)**: Update \`cass robot-docs paths\` to tell the truth. Replace the \`log path\` line with something like:
   \`\`\`
   log path (TUI only): <data-dir>/cass.log.YYYY-MM-DD (daily rolling)
   trace: user-provided path via CASS_TRACE_FILE or --trace-file (JSONL)
   \`\`\`
   Update \`cass doctor\` hint text at src/lib.rs:13620 to remove the \"check cass.log\" reference unless doctor actually writes one (or add logging to doctor).

2. **Behavior fix (larger)**: Install the rolling-daily file appender for every long-running command (at least \`index\`, \`search\`, \`doctor\`, \`models install\`, \`models backfill\`) so the advertised log actually materializes. Keep the quick one-shot commands (\`health\`, \`api-version\`, \`diag\`) on stderr-only to avoid creating noisy files for 50ms invocations.

Option 1 is the smaller-risk fix and truthfully reflects shipped behavior. Option 2 is more useful but needs a broader review of what to log and where.

Severity: MEDIUM — a documented artifact that isn't there breaks triage assumptions; corrected docs restore operator trust without code changes.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1w3zv","title":"Fix End key and Page Down scroll boundary in conversation detail view (GH#67)","description":"## Problem

In the conversation detail view of the TUI, pressing End blanks the pane and Page Down scrolls past content bounds into blank space.

## Root Cause

In `src/ui/app.rs:10371-10379`, the scroll handlers use `.max(0)` for lower bound but enforce no upper bound on `detail_scroll`:

```rust
CassMsg::DetailScrolled { delta } => {
    let new_scroll = self.detail_scroll as i32 + delta;
    self.detail_scroll = new_scroll.max(0) as u16;  // No upper bound!
}
CassMsg::PageScrolled { delta } => {
    let new_scroll = self.detail_scroll as i32 + (delta * 20);
    self.detail_scroll = new_scroll.max(0) as u16;  // No upper bound!
}
```

The rendering code at line 5979-5985 does clamp at render time:
```rust
let effective_scroll = scroll.min(total_lines.saturating_sub(1));
```

But `total_lines` at render time is the number of rendered lines, which can be 0 if the scroll offset exceeds content. The End key (line 9193: `CassMsg::CursorJumped { to_end: true }`) maps to selection jump, not detail scroll -- it likely moves the issue list cursor to the end, not the detail pane scroll.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add max scroll clamping to scroll handlers (app.rs:10371-10379)

```rust
CassMsg::DetailScrolled { delta } => {
    let new_scroll = self.detail_scroll as i32 + delta;
    let max_scroll = self.detail_content_lines.saturating_sub(visible_height) as i32;
    self.detail_scroll = new_scroll.clamp(0, max_scroll.max(0)) as u16;
}
```

This requires knowing `detail_content_lines` (total rendered lines) and `visible_height` (detail pane height). Store these from the last render pass.

### 2. Add detail_content_lines tracking (app.rs state)

Add field to App struct near `detail_scroll` (line 3339):
```rust
pub detail_content_lines: u16,
pub detail_visible_height: u16,
```

Update these during detail rendering (line 5979-5985 area) after computing `total_lines` and `visible_height`.

### 3. Handle End key in detail pane context (app.rs:9190-9196)

When focus is on the detail pane, End should scroll to the bottom:
```rust
KeyCode::End if self.focused_region() == FocusRegion::Detail => {
    self.detail_scroll = self.detail_content_lines.saturating_sub(self.detail_visible_height);
    Cmd::none()
}
```

### 4. Handle Home key in detail pane context

When focus is on detail pane, Home should scroll to top:
```rust
KeyCode::Home if self.focused_region() == FocusRegion::Detail => {
    self.detail_scroll = 0;
    Cmd::none()
}
```

## Files to Modify

- `src/ui/app.rs` lines 3339-3343 (add tracking fields)
- `src/ui/app.rs` lines 5979-5985 (update tracking during render)
- `src/ui/app.rs` lines 9190-9196 (End/Home key handlers for detail)
- `src/ui/app.rs` lines 10371-10379 (add upper bound clamping)","acceptance_criteria":"1. End key scrolls detail pane to bottom of content (last lines visible)
2. End key does NOT produce blank/empty pane
3. Home key scrolls detail pane to top
4. Page Down stops at content bottom (no blank space below last line)
5. Page Up stops at top (scroll = 0)
6. Mouse wheel scrolling also respects bounds
7. j/k vim-style scrolling respects bounds
8. Scroll position clamped when content changes (e.g., switching to shorter conversation)
9. Works correctly when content fits entirely in pane (no scrolling needed)","notes":"## Unit Tests

1. **test_detail_scroll_upper_bound**: Set content_lines=100, visible_height=20. Scroll delta +200. Assert detail_scroll = 80 (not 200).
2. **test_detail_scroll_lower_bound**: Set detail_scroll=50. Scroll delta -100. Assert detail_scroll = 0 (not negative).
3. **test_end_key_scrolls_to_bottom**: Simulate End key in detail focus. Assert detail_scroll = content_lines - visible_height.
4. **test_home_key_scrolls_to_top**: Set detail_scroll=50. Simulate Home in detail focus. Assert detail_scroll = 0.
5. **test_page_down_clamped**: Set content_lines=30, visible_height=20, scroll=15. Page Down (delta=20). Assert scroll=10 (max).
6. **test_short_content_no_scroll**: Set content_lines=10, visible_height=20. Any scroll. Assert scroll stays 0.
7. **test_content_switch_reclamps**: Set scroll=80 (for 100 lines). Switch to conversation with 30 lines. Assert scroll clamped to max(0, 30-visible).

## Manual E2E Test Procedure

1. Launch `cass` TUI
2. Select a long conversation in the list
3. Press Tab to focus detail pane
4. Press End -- should show last lines of conversation, not blank
5. Press Home -- should return to top
6. Press Page Down repeatedly -- should stop at bottom, no blank space
7. Press Page Up repeatedly -- should stop at top
8. Select a short conversation -- detail scroll should reset/clamp
9. Mouse wheel in detail pane -- should respect bounds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:03.821527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:10:31.497456Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:10:31.497373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1wnh","title":"Add CI validation for E2E logging compliance","description":"## Priority 2: Enhance CI Validation for E2E Logging Compliance

### Current State
- scripts/validate-e2e-jsonl.sh ALREADY EXISTS for JSONL validation
- scripts/validate_ci.sh ALREADY EXISTS for general CI checks
- Missing: E2E test file compliance check (PhaseTracker usage)

### Required Changes

1. **Add E2E logging compliance check to validate_ci.sh:**

Add a new check section:
```bash
# ============================================================
# E2E Logging Compliance Check
# ============================================================
echo "X. Checking E2E logging compliance..."

E2E_ERRORS=0

# Check that all e2e_*.rs files import e2e_log module
for f in tests/e2e_*.rs; do
    name=$(basename "$f")
    
    # Check for e2e_log import
    if ! grep -q "use.*e2e_log\|mod.*e2e_log" "$f"; then
        echo "  ERROR: $name missing e2e_log import"
        ((E2E_ERRORS++))
    fi
    
    # Check for PhaseTracker usage (after consolidation is complete)
    if ! grep -q "PhaseTracker" "$f"; then
        echo "  WARNING: $name not using PhaseTracker (may need migration)"
    fi
done

# Check shell scripts
for f in scripts/e2e/*.sh; do
    if [ -f "$f" ] && [ -s "$f" ]; then
        name=$(basename "$f")
        if ! grep -q "e2e_log.sh" "$f"; then
            echo "  WARNING: $name not sourcing e2e_log.sh"
        fi
    fi
done

if [ $E2E_ERRORS -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "  FAILED: $E2E_ERRORS E2E logging compliance error(s)"
    exit 1
fi
echo "  OK: E2E logging compliance checks passed"
```

2. **Add CI workflow step for JSONL validation:**

In .github/workflows/test.yml:
```yaml
- name: Validate E2E JSONL logs
  if: always()
  run: |
    if ls test-results/e2e/*.jsonl 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then
      ./scripts/validate-e2e-jsonl.sh test-results/e2e/*.jsonl
    else
      echo "No JSONL files to validate (tests may have been skipped)"
    fi
```

### Files to Modify
- scripts/validate_ci.sh (add E2E compliance check)
- .github/workflows/test.yml (add JSONL validation step)

### NOTE: DO NOT create scripts/validate_e2e_logging.sh
Use existing validate_ci.sh instead.

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)

1. **Test compliance check catches issues:**
```bash
# Create non-compliant file (temporarily)
echo 'fn test() {}' > /tmp/test_e2e_bad.rs
./scripts/validate_ci.sh  # Should warn about missing imports
rm /tmp/test_e2e_bad.rs
```

2. **Test JSONL validation works:**
```bash
# Run tests to generate JSONL
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_search_index -- --nocapture
./scripts/validate-e2e-jsonl.sh test-results/e2e/*.jsonl
echo "Exit code: $?"  # Should be 0
```

3. **Test with invalid JSONL:**
```bash
echo '{"invalid": true}' > test-results/e2e/bad.jsonl
./scripts/validate-e2e-jsonl.sh test-results/e2e/bad.jsonl || echo "Correctly rejected"
rm test-results/e2e/bad.jsonl
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] validate_ci.sh checks E2E test file compliance
- [ ] validate_ci.sh warns about missing PhaseTracker usage
- [ ] validate_ci.sh checks shell scripts source e2e_log.sh
- [ ] CI workflow runs validate-e2e-jsonl.sh on JSONL output
- [ ] CI fails if JSONL validation fails

**Parent Phase:** Phase 5: Polish & Safety
**Section Reference:** Plan Document Section 14 (Guardrail 3)
**Depends On:** P1.1 (Database Export)

## Goal

Before export, scan all conversation content for potential secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys) and alert the user.

## Technical Approach

### Secret Patterns (Regex)

```rust
use regex::Regex;
use lazy_static::lazy_static;

lazy_static! {
    static ref SECRET_PATTERNS: Vec<(&'static str, Regex)> = vec![
        // OpenAI API keys
        ("OpenAI API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}").unwrap()),
        
        // GitHub tokens
        ("GitHub PAT", Regex::new(r"ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        ("GitHub OAuth", Regex::new(r"gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        
        // AWS
        ("AWS Access Key", Regex::new(r"AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}").unwrap()),
        ("AWS Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)aws[_-]?secret[_-]?access[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic patterns
        ("API Key", Regex::new(r"(?i)api[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)secret\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Password", Regex::new(r"(?i)password\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[^\s'\"]{8,}").unwrap()),
        ("Private Key", Regex::new(r"-----BEGIN (RSA |EC |DSA |OPENSSH )?PRIVATE KEY-----").unwrap()),
        
        // Anthropic
        ("Anthropic API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9-_]{40,}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic high-entropy
        ("Bearer Token", Regex::new(r"(?i)bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9._-]{20,}").unwrap()),
    ];
}
```

### Entropy-Based Detection

```rust
/// Calculate Shannon entropy of a string
fn shannon_entropy(s: &str) -> f64 {
    let mut freq = [0u32; 256];
    let len = s.len() as f64;
    
    for b in s.bytes() {
        freq[b as usize] += 1;
    }
    
    freq.iter()
        .filter(|&&c| c > 0)
        .map(|&c| {
            let p = c as f64 / len;
            -p * p.log2()
        })
        .sum()
}

/// Detect high-entropy strings (potential secrets)
fn detect_high_entropy_secrets(content: &str) -> Vec<SecretMatch> {
    let mut matches = Vec::new();
    
    // Look for base64-like strings of 20+ chars
    let base64_re = Regex::new(r"[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}").unwrap();
    
    for m in base64_re.find_iter(content) {
        let s = m.as_str();
        let entropy = shannon_entropy(s);
        
        // High entropy (>4.5 bits per char) suggests random/secret data
        if entropy > 4.5 && s.len() >= 20 {
            matches.push(SecretMatch {
                pattern_name: "High-Entropy String".to_string(),
                matched_text: truncate_secret(s, 20),
                line_number: count_newlines(&content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                entropy: Some(entropy),
            });
        }
    }
    
    matches
}
```

### Scanner Implementation

```rust
pub struct SecretMatch {
    pub pattern_name: String,
    pub matched_text: String, // Truncated/masked
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub context: String,      // Surrounding text
    pub conversation_id: i64,
    pub message_idx: i64,
    pub entropy: Option<f64>,
}

pub struct ScanResult {
    pub matches: Vec<SecretMatch>,
    pub conversations_scanned: usize,
    pub messages_scanned: usize,
    pub scan_duration_ms: u64,
}

/// Scan all messages for potential secrets
pub fn scan_for_secrets(db: &Database, filter: &ExportFilter) -> Result<ScanResult> {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut all_matches = Vec::new();
    let mut msg_count = 0;
    let mut conv_count = 0;
    
    let conversations = db.query_filtered_conversations(filter)?;
    
    for conv in &conversations {
        conv_count += 1;
        let messages = db.get_conversation_messages(conv.id)?;
        
        for msg in messages {
            msg_count += 1;
            
            // Pattern matching
            for (name, pattern) in SECRET_PATTERNS.iter() {
                for m in pattern.find_iter(&msg.content) {
                    all_matches.push(SecretMatch {
                        pattern_name: name.to_string(),
                        matched_text: truncate_secret(m.as_str(), 20),
                        line_number: count_newlines(&msg.content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                        context: extract_context(&msg.content, m.start(), m.end(), 50),
                        conversation_id: conv.id,
                        message_idx: msg.idx,
                        entropy: None,
                    });
                }
            }
            
            // Entropy detection
            all_matches.extend(detect_high_entropy_secrets(&msg.content));
        }
    }
    
    Ok(ScanResult {
        matches: all_matches,
        conversations_scanned: conv_count,
        messages_scanned: msg_count,
        scan_duration_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
    })
}
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","title":"Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes","description":"BACKGROUND:
Once the pipeline is packet-driven, cass can stop recomputing identical derived work for repeated content. Historical salvage, replayed sessions, repeated assistant boilerplate, and semantic rebuilds all create opportunities for content-addressed reuse.

GOAL:
Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes.

SCOPE:
- Key memoization by stable content hash plus algorithm or version fingerprint.
- Reuse cached results for lexical normalization artifacts, token extraction or model normalization, semantic embeddings, and other demonstrably expensive pure derivations.
- Keep cache storage, invalidation, and budget rules explicit so future agents can reason about hits, misses, and evictions.

DONE WHEN:
Repeated content stops paying full derived-work cost on every refresh without risking stale or cross-version incorrectness.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Memoization keys combine stable packet hash with algorithm or version fingerprint so incompatible derivations cannot be silently reused.
- Cache behavior is operator-auditable through structured hit, miss, invalidation, eviction, quarantine, and budget logs, with explicit storage and retention rules.
- Unit, integration, and robot or E2E scenarios cover repeated-content refreshes, version bumps, cache invalidation, sampled recompute or compare-mode verification, suspected corruption or stale-entry quarantine, and bounded storage so memoization improves real workloads without silent stale-output risk.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-23T15:43:09.195565970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","caching","indexing","performance","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:51.822523343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:15.428873220Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":516,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- Memoization is only worth doing for pure or version-fingerprinted derivations; do not cache hidden side effects.
- Budget and eviction policy must be explicit and inspectable, not mystery state.
- Prefer cache entries validated by packet hash and algorithm version rather than ad hoc heuristics.
- Acceptance requires observable cache hits, misses, and invalidation reasons plus proof that version changes cannot silently reuse incompatible outputs.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11Z"},{"id":527,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"ubuntu","text":"POLISH ROUND 3:
- Added a direct dependency on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31 because stable packet hashes and packet-version semantics are not merely transitive implementation detail; they are the conceptual foundation of safe content-addressed memoization.
- The acceptance bar now explicitly includes cache auditability and repeated-content E2E scenarios so memoization serves users through faster refreshes without creating mystery state.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:55Z"},{"id":639,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"cc_2","text":"2026-04-22: Shipped content-addressed memoization vocabulary slice (commit 549e10f5).

What landed in src/indexer/memoization.rs (newly created):
- MemoContentHash — opaque byte-fingerprint carrier; producer-owned hasher choice.
- MemoKey { content_hash, algorithm, algorithm_version } — the core invariant: any version bump of any derivation invalidates its prior cache entries because the composite key differs. Silent stale cross-version reuse is impossible by construction.
- MemoLookup<V> — Hit / Miss / Quarantined variants, snake_case serialized for structured audit logs.
- MemoCacheEvent — Hit / Miss / Insert / Evict{CapacityLru|Invalidated} / Quarantine{reason} / Invalidate unified vocabulary for downstream logs.
- MemoCacheStats — hits, misses, inserts, evictions_capacity, invalidations, quarantined, live_entries counters; snake_case serializable.
- ContentAddressedMemoCache<V> — bounded LRU, quarantine-aware in-memory cache:
  - capacity_lru eviction when max_entries is reached (touched entries stay resident).
  - Quarantined entries remain resident (operator inspection) but never serve a hit; re-insertion over a quarantined key is a noop that returns Quarantine{reason}.
  - invalidate() returns true only when an entry was actually removed, so counter bumps are honest.

Nine unit tests, all passing:
- memo_key_distinguishes_by_content_algorithm_and_version
- memo_key_round_trips_through_json
- empty_cache_returns_miss_and_records_stat
- insert_then_get_returns_hit_and_bumps_counters
- version_bump_does_not_hit_prior_entry
- capacity_lru_evicts_oldest_and_reports_event
- invalidate_removes_entry_and_bumps_counter
- quarantined_entry_stays_resident_but_never_hits
- stats_serialize_as_snake_case_and_count_live_entries

Module is dead-code tolerated; nothing in the rebuild pipeline consumes it yet.

Bead stays in_progress. Remaining slices for closure:
- Wire ContentAddressedMemoCache into the lexical normalization, token extraction, and semantic-embedding paths once the ConversationPacket dataflow migration (ibuuh.32) lands and the hot derivations are factored through packets.
- Expose structured tracing::info!/warn! events for every MemoCacheEvent variant (hit/miss/insert/evict/quarantine/invalidate) at the wiring sites.
- Integration tests for the repeated-content refresh, version-bump invalidation, sampled recompute / compare-mode verification, and bounded-storage scenarios mandated by AC3.
- Robot / E2E scenario proving repeated refreshes on an unchanged corpus stop paying full derived-work cost.
","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:53:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.35","title":"Extend the telemetry-driven control layer across segment-farm rebuild, memoization budgets, and unified refresh policy","description":"BACKGROUND:

GOAL:

- Keep the unified controller explainable and conservative, with explicit policy logs and safe disable or pin options for operators.
- Ensure controller behavior respects user-visible latency, machine pressure, and recovery safety across watch, rebuild, and maintenance flows.

Cass has one explicit, auditable control surface that governs serial and parallel refresh behavior, memoization budgets, and degraded-mode selection without compromising correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.37","title":"Introduce a conservative telemetry-driven controller for the improved serial rebuild and publish path","description":"BACKGROUND:
The improved serial rebuild and generation publish path already has enough telemetry surfaces to benefit from explicit adaptation before the full segment-farm architecture lands. Waiting until the end to replace static thresholds leaves avoidable throughput and stability wins on the table and makes later parallel control harder to tune.

GOAL:
Introduce a conservative, explainable controller for the improved serial rebuild and generation publish path.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-y4by","title":"[INFRA] Regression Guardrails and CI Benchmarking","description":"# Infrastructure: Regression Guardrails and CI Benchmarking

## Purpose

Ensure performance optimizations don't regress over time. This task sets up:
1. Automated benchmark comparison in CI
2. Performance thresholds that fail the build
3. Baseline tracking across commits

## Current State

### Existing Guardrails
- `tests/robot_perf.rs`: Latency thresholds for robot commands
- `tests/cli_robot.rs:334`: Sessions output metamorphic parity
- `src/search/tantivy.rs:785`: title_prefix matching test

These are correctness tests, not performance regression tests.

### Missing
- Automated benchmark comparison in CI
- Baseline storage and tracking
- Threshold-based failure for regressions

## Proposed Solution

### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow for Benchmarks

```yaml
# .github/workflows/perf.yml
name: Performance Benchmarks

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  bench:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust (nightly)
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@nightly
        
      - name: Restore baseline
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: target/criterion
          key: bench-baseline-${{ github.base_ref }}
          
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline pr
        
      - name: Compare to baseline
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          cargo install critcmp
          critcmp main pr --threshold 10
          # Fails if any benchmark regresses by >10%
          
      - name: Save baseline (on merge to main)
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline main
```

### 2. Critical Benchmark Thresholds

| Benchmark | Threshold | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| `search_latency` | < 50 µs | TUI responsiveness |
| `vector_index_search_50k` | < 10 ms (after opts) | Semantic search target |
| `index_small_batch` | < 20 ms | Indexing throughput |
| `canonicalize_long_message` | < 500 µs (after opt) | Index-time target |

### 3. Memory Regression Tests

```rust
// tests/perf_memory.rs
use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

struct TrackingAllocator;

static ALLOCATED: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);
static PEAK: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);

#[global_allocator]
static ALLOC: TrackingAllocator = TrackingAllocator;

unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator {
    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
        let ptr = System.alloc(layout);
        if !ptr.is_null() {
            let current = ALLOCATED.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst) + layout.size();
            PEAK.fetch_max(current, Ordering::SeqCst);
        }
        ptr
    }

    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.dealloc(ptr, layout)
    }
}

#[test]
fn indexing_peak_memory_regression() {
    // Index test corpus
    let _stats = index_test_corpus();
    
    let peak_mb = PEAK.load(Ordering::SeqCst) / 1_000_000;
    assert!(peak_mb < 350, "Peak memory {}MB exceeds 350MB threshold", peak_mb);
}
```

### 4. Benchmark Result Artifact Collection

```yaml
- name: Upload benchmark results
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: benchmark-results
    path: target/criterion/**/*.json
    retention-days: 30
```

This enables historical analysis and trend detection.

## Implementation Checklist

1. [ ] Create `.github/workflows/perf.yml`
2. [ ] Add `critcmp` comparison step
3. [ ] Set appropriate thresholds for each benchmark
4. [ ] Add memory tracking test
5. [ ] Configure artifact retention
6. [ ] Add PR comment bot for benchmark diffs (optional)
7. [ ] Document threshold rationale in README

## Validation Commands

After implementing, verify with:
```bash
# Run benchmarks locally
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline before

# Make a change
# ...

# Compare
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- --save-baseline after
cargo install critcmp && critcmp before after
```
